verdicts.expected: BOYCON74 is conical buoy,gw,full-chart. Required geometry: conical/nun buoy body. Required colours: green, white, green, white, green, all visible in order inside the cone with no external scaffolding.
verdicts.judge_comments: The repair fixed the protruding-stroke artifact but did not make all load-bearing colour bands visually legible against the OpenCPN/source-priority witnesses and semantic brief. Not final-approved.
verdicts.observed: Conical/nun body is clipped cleanly and the previous external separator bars are gone, but the visible symbol still reads as white-green-white with only tiny apex/base green slivers. It does not clearly preserve the required green-white-green-white-green five-band sequence.
verdicts.required_change: Redraw or proportion the conical body so the five ordered green-white-green-white-green bands are visibly present inside the cone; keep the clipping/no-external-strokes fix.

verdicts.expected: BOYCON81 is conical buoy,gw,full-chart. Required geometry: conical/nun buoy body. Required colours: blue, red, white, blue with the special-purpose horizontal/vertical stripe treatment clear enough to distinguish the intended S-52 symbol.
verdicts.judge_comments: The repair removed external separator spill but the mixed stripe contract is still not visually resolved. A special-purpose conical buoy with this pattern is safety-sensitive because a muddled stripe layout can read as the wrong buoy variant. Not final-approved.
verdicts.observed: Conical/nun body is clipped and includes blue/red/white/blue colours, but the semi-transparent vertical overlays and internal grid produce a muddy cross-stripe pattern that still does not match the OpenCPN witness or the simpler source-priority rendering.
verdicts.required_change: Redraw BOYCON81 against the OpenCPN/reference witness so the blue-red-white-blue horizontal/vertical stripe pattern is unambiguous; remove any internal grid lines or translucent overlays that are not part of the reference symbol.

verdicts.expected: BOYPIL78 is a special-purpose pillar buoy. Required geometry: pillar buoy body. Required colours: red and white with a squared/checkered pattern in the listed colour order.
verdicts.judge_comments: The candidate preserves the required pillar class and red/white checkered visual cue while staying within the silhouette. Styling remains Helm-owned and this is not final approval.
verdicts.observed: Pillar buoy body with red/white checkered squares clipped inside the silhouette, black outline, and stem; the previous external checker/grid spill is gone.
verdicts.required_change: No repair required for this visual rerun; keep pending final approval QA and do not mark final-approved.

verdicts.expected: BOYSPH79 requires a resolved reference contract before approval: if the semantic brief/S-57 BOYSHP1 mapping is authoritative, the symbol should be a conical/nun red-over-green buoy; if the S-101/Chart No.1 witnesses are authoritative, the row mapping must be corrected before visual pass.
verdicts.judge_comments: This is semantically improved, but the reference set conflicts with the repaired candidate and lacks the OpenCPN render needed to arbitrate the class/shape. Do not promote while reference evidence disagrees. Not final-approved.
verdicts.observed: Candidate now uses a conical/nun red-over-green body, fixing the prior spherical Helm render relative to the semantic brief. However, the OpenCPN reference render is absent locally and the available source-priority/Chart No.1 witnesses still show a circular/spherical red-green cue, so visual parity cannot be proven.
verdicts.required_change: Regenerate or attach the exact OpenCPN/S-101 reference for BOYSPH79 and reconcile the row's conical-versus-spherical contract; then rerun visual judging before any pass.
